title: AdditivePlatformMachineElement Object
description: Machine element representing the additive platform settings.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: staticTypeId
methods.description: Identifying name for all elements of this type. Pass this to the itemByType or itemById methods of MachineElements to filter to elements of this type.

properties.name: ceilingClearance
properties.description: Clearance height used for automatically arranging parts which is the distance from the top of the build platform. Units are cm.

properties.name: clearance
properties.description: Clearance height used for automatically arranging parts and suggested height for positioning part on the build platform. Units are cm.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.description: Radius used to round the corners of the build platform. Units are cm.

properties.name: elementId
properties.description: Identifier for this element. Unique within an element type.

properties.name: frameWidth
properties.description: Clearance width used for automatically arranging parts which is the distance from the edges of the build platform. Units are cm.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Origin point specifying the platform coordinates that correspond to the origin of the platform mesh. Units are cm.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Usable platform size. Units are cm.

properties.name: typeId
properties.description: Identifier for this type of machine element.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

ceilingClearance.title: AdditivePlatformMachineElement.ceilingClearance Property
ceilingClearance.description: Clearance height used for automatically arranging parts which is the distance from the top of the build platform. Units are cm.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = additivePlatformMachineElement_var.ceilingClearance

# Set the value of the property.
additivePlatformMachineElement_var.ceilingClearance = propertyValue

clearance.description: Clearance height used for automatically arranging parts and suggested height for positioning part on the build platform. Units are cm.

syntax.python:
